meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Use this intermediate checklist to master idea validation using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio with confidence.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project abandonment requires aggressive idea validation. By constraining your scope to a single, high-value feature and iterating with AI-assisted workflows, you transform abstract concepts into shipping code. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting of implementation, ensuring you hit milestones consistently.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished code is littered with ambitious ideas that lacked a heartbeat. You start with high-octane motivation, but somewhere between the database schema and the third API integration, the friction of 'not knowing what to do next' turns your excitement into technical debt.

But here is the secret: building isn't about endurance; it's about engineering constraints. By shifting your focus from 'building an app' to 'validating a solution,' you turn the chaos of development into a predictable, mechanical process. Let’s strip away the fluff and build your first production-ready milestone.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify the single, most painful problem your tool solves. If you can't describe it in one sentence, your scope is too broad.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into the smallest possible functional unit that provides immediate user value. Identify the core 'Aha!' moment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'What is the minimum viable feature set for [Idea] that I can build in 48 hours?'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Map how data moves from user input to output. This prevents 'feature creep' by keeping the backend lean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean architecture diagram and database schema for [Idea]. Focus on 3 core tables and prioritize speed of implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Generate a schema.prisma file based on the core data flow identified in step 1.'

steps.title: Build the MVP Skeleton
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold your project so you aren't staring at a blank terminal. Focus on the happy path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack Next.js + Tailwind project for [Idea]. Include only the necessary boilerplate for authentication and the primary CRUD operation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Initialize the project structure using the scaffolding provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic in isolation. Test each function before integrating it into the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic function for [Feature] in TypeScript. Include error handling and type definitions for edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement the function for [Feature] and write unit tests for the core logic.'

steps.title: Validate with Real-World Feedback
steps.description: Deploy to a staging environment and get one user to test it. Their friction is your roadmap for the next iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist of 5 critical success metrics for [Idea] to evaluate if the MVP solves the user's problem effectively.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'How can I instrument this code to track user drop-off points during the core workflow?'

pro_tips: Never build more than 3 screens for your first MVP; if you need more, you are building a product, not a project.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code every 2 hours to prevent technical debt from slowing your momentum.
If you find yourself stuck for more than 30 minutes, switch to a different component rather than debugging the same line.
Always document the 'why' behind your architectural choices in a README; it turns your project into a professional case study.
Treat your first version as disposable; the goal is to learn the stack, not to create a permanent masterpiece.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share it with three peers for feedback, iterate on the top complaint, and then add this project to your portfolio with a summary of the problem you solved.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you write today is an investment in your career as a world-class AI builder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
